item.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump says “the weaponization fund is a great idea” and if Republicans “don't get it approved, I'd be disappointed.” Asked whether anyone who attacked police officers on January 6 should get taxpayer money, Trump says, “I wouldn't be inclined to say so, but I have to see it.”

media:content.media:title: Trump says ‘I’d pay’ anti-weaponization fund applicants ‘the kind of money they deserve’
media:content.media:description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Trump says “the weaponization fund is a great idea” and if Republicans “don't get it approved, I'd be disappointed.” Asked whether anyone who attacked police officers on January 6 should get taxpayer money, Trump says, “I wouldn't be inclined to say so, but I have to see it.”

item.title: Trump says ‘I’d pay’ anti-weaponization fund applicants ‘the kind of money they deserve’

item.description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to share key takeaways her exclusive interview with President Donald Trump about the war in Iran, its impact on the economy and his claims of voter fraud in California and the 2020 election. Kristen says, “I pressed him on his promise not to get the United States engaged in foreign wars. He said ‘This is not going to be a forever war.’ He insists it’s not a quagmire.”

media:content.media:title: Trump on Pressure to End Iran War With Peace Deal
media:content.media:description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to share key takeaways her exclusive interview with President Donald Trump about the war in Iran, its impact on the economy and his claims of voter fraud in California and the 2020 election. Kristen says, “I pressed him on his promise not to get the United States engaged in foreign wars. He said ‘This is not going to be a forever war.’ He insists it’s not a quagmire.”

item.title: Trump on Pressure to End Iran War With Peace Deal

item.dc:creator: Peter Nicholas
item.description: President Donald Trump did not rule out the government paying people who were charged with assaulting police officers during the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S.

media:content.media:text: Meet The Press- President Trump
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" moderator Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wis.

item.title: Trump doesn’t rule out giving Jan. 6 rioters who attacked police ‘anti-weaponization’ fund payouts

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
item.description: Digging into some of the president’s claims on the Iran war, the Jan. 6 riots, California’s primary elections and more.

media:content.media:text: "Meet the Press" Host Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wisc.
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" moderator Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026, at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wis.

item.title: Fact-checking Trump’s interview with NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: At least 12 people were shot Saturday when gunmen, possibly aiming for each other, opened fire near the Old West End Festival in Toledo, Ohio, police said.

media:content.media:text: Deputy Chief Joseph Heffernan, of the Toledo, Ohio, police department, provides details about a shooting that left at least 12 people wounded Saturday night.
media:content.media:description: Deputy Chief Joseph Heffernan, of the Toledo, Ohio, police department, provides details about a shooting that left at least 12 people wounded Saturday night.

item.title: At least 12 shot near Ohio festival, gunmen still at large

item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero
item.description: U.S. gymnastics great Simone Biles shared limited news of a health scare on Saturday, an experience she said included “almost dying.”The world’s most decorated gymnast said on Instagram Stories that she had endured an unexpected situation, and she thanked well-wishers.“I’m not one to normally share things like this because I value privacy in today’s age,” Biles, 29, said on the social media platform.

media:content.media:text: Simone Biles attends the Laureus World Sports Awards 2026.
media:content.media:description: Simone Biles.

item.title: Simone Biles reveals she had a health scare and describes ‘almost dying’

item.description: A new luxury development in Albania, backed by Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump, is drawing backlash from locals citing environmental concerns. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports.

media:content.media:title: Backlash in Albania over plans for new luxury resort backed by Kushner
media:content.media:description: A new luxury development in Albania, backed by Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump, is drawing backlash from locals citing environmental concerns. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports.

item.title: Backlash in Albania over plans for new luxury resort backed by Kushner

item.dc:creator: Matt Lavietes
Maria Guerrero, NBC Dallas-Fort Worth
Dennis Romero
item.description: A Texas medical examiner testified at the Karmelo Anthony murder trial Saturday, saying that the 17-year-old victim, Austin Metcalf, was stabbed through the heart.

media:content.media:text: A courtroom sketch shows the district attorney pointing at Karmelo Anthony, center, at the defense table in opening arguments on June 4, 2026, in McKinney, Texas, during the trial of a teen accused of fatally stabbing another during a track meet in suburban Dallas last year.
media:content.media:description: A courtroom sketch shows the district attorney pointing at Karmelo Anthony, center, at the defense table during opening arguments in McKinney, Texas, on Thursday. Anthony is accused of fatally stabbing another teenager during a track meet last year.

item.title: Teen witnesses describe scene at track meet in Karmelo Anthony murder trial

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: All eyes will be on the World Cup host cities this summer, but perhaps none more so than Los Angeles, which is also preparing to host the 2028 Summer Olympics

media:content.media:text: Workers affix FIFA World Cup 2026 logos and signage on a fence outside of SoFi Stadium
media:content.media:description: Workers affix FIFA World Cup 2026 logos and signage at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., on May 26.

media:content.media:text: The interior of SoFi Stadium
media:content.media:description: Workers affix FIFA World Cup 2026 logos and signage at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., on May 26.

item.title: World Cup gives Los Angeles a chance to showcase Olympic readiness

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Allen
item.description: President Donald Trump said the U.S. will work with Iran to retrieve and destroy its highly enriched uranium if he is able to cut a deal with Tehran to end the war.

media:content.media:text: "Meet the Press" Host Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wisc.
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" moderator Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wis.

item.title: Trump says the U.S. will work with Iran to destroy its uranium if they can make a deal

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
item.description: Digging into some of the president’s claims on the Iran war, the Jan. 6 riots, California’s primary elections and more.

media:content.media:text: "Meet the Press" Host Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026 at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wisc.
media:content.media:description: "Meet the Press" moderator Kristen Welker interviews President Donald Trump on June 5, 2026, at Custer Farms in Chippewa Falls, Wis.

item.title: Fact-checking Trump’s interview with NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: All eyes will be on the World Cup host cities this summer, but perhaps none more so than Los Angeles, which is also preparing to host the 2028 Summer Olympics

media:content.media:text: Workers affix FIFA World Cup 2026 logos and signage on a fence outside of SoFi Stadium
media:content.media:description: Workers affix FIFA World Cup 2026 logos and signage at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., on May 26.

media:content.media:description: Workers affix FIFA World Cup 2026 logos and signage at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., on May 26.

item.dc:creator: NBC News
item.description: NBC News’ chief data analyst Steve Kornacki will answer NBC News subscriber questions about the midterm elections and more in a live Q&A on Tuesday at 12 p.m. ET.

media:content.media:text: Steve Kornacki in a studio at NBC News.
media:content.media:description: Steve Kornacki.

item.title: Send your questions for Steve Kornacki

item.dc:creator: Evan Bush
item.description: It took the U.S. decades and hundreds of millions of dollars to eradicate the flesh-eating New World screwworm. Here’s the plan to renew the fight now that it’s back.

media:content.media:text: Screwworm Livestock
media:content.media:description: A test container of dyed fly pupae are displayed on Feb. 9 at a facility in Edinburg, Texas, that produces sterile New World screwworms as part of the effort to stop their spread and protect American livestock.

media:content.media:text: U.S. Cattle Herd Drops To 75-Year Low Amid Challenges For Ranchers
media:content.media:description: A test container of dyed fly pupae are displayed on Feb. 9 at a facility in Edinburg, Texas, that produces sterile New World screwworms as part of the effort to stop their spread and protect American livestock.

item.title: The U.S. fought the flesh-eating screwworm for decades. Now it must begin again.

item.dc:creator: Erin Tan
Jennifer Jett
item.description: As temperatures neared their midday peak, Hu Xiaosong was doing his regular patrol, at an altitude of more than 5,500 feet, to protect a 1,000-year-old pine tree that he says is like family to him.

media:content.media:text: Hu Xiaochun guardian of the tree in Huangshan, China.
media:content.media:description: Hu Xiaosong, guardian of the tree, in Huangshan, China, in 2018.

media:content.media:text: Welcoming-Guests Pine in in Huangshan Mountain, China.
media:content.media:description: Hu Xiaosong, guardian of the tree, in Huangshan, China, in 2018.

media:content.media:text: Tourists in Huangshan, China.
media:content.media:description: Hu Xiaosong, guardian of the tree, in Huangshan, China, in 2018.

media:content.media:text: Hu Xiaosong in Huangshan, China, this year.
media:content.media:description: Hu Xiaosong, guardian of the tree, in Huangshan, China, in 2018.

media:content.media:text: Huangshan, China
media:content.media:description: Hu Xiaosong, guardian of the tree, in Huangshan, China, in 2018.

media:content.media:text: Huangshan Mountain, China.
media:content.media:description: Hu Xiaosong, guardian of the tree, in Huangshan, China, in 2018.

item.title: This 1,000-year-old pine tree’s protector fears changing weather patterns

item.dc:creator: Mithil Aggarwal
Raf Sanchez
Mo Abbas
item.description: President Donald Trump’s son-in-law has a formidable new opponent: a pink flamingo

media:content.media:text: Albania Protest Kushner
media:content.media:description: Protesters hold pink flamingo cutouts during a demonstration in Tirana, Albania, Wednesday, June 3, 2026, opposing a luxury coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump over concerns about environmental impacts and transparency.

media:content.media:text: Protests against tourism project continue in Albania
media:content.media:description: Thousands of demonstrators march Thursday in Tirana to protest the development project.

media:content.media:text: Fourth Day Of Protests Against The Narta Lagoon Development Project
media:content.media:description: Police deploy a water cannon against demonstrators protesting the project in Tirana on Wednesday.

item.title: Why a luxury resort plan by Trump’s son-in-law sparked mass protests in Albania

item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero
item.description: U.S. gymnastics great Simone Biles shared limited news of a health scare on Saturday, an experience she said included “almost dying.”The world’s most decorated gymnast said on Instagram Stories that she had endured an unexpected situation, and she thanked well-wishers.“I’m not one to normally share things like this because I value privacy in today’s age,” Biles, 29, said on the social media platform.

media:content.media:text: Simone Biles attends the Laureus World Sports Awards 2026.
media:content.media:description: Simone Biles.

item.title: Simone Biles reveals she had a health scare and describes ‘almost dying’

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: At least 12 people were shot Saturday when gunmen, possibly aiming for each other, opened fire near the Old West End Festival in Toledo, Ohio, police said.

media:content.media:text: Deputy Chief Joseph Heffernan, of the Toledo, Ohio, police department, provides details about a shooting that left at least 12 people wounded Saturday night.
media:content.media:description: Deputy Chief Joseph Heffernan, of the Toledo, Ohio, police department, provides details about a shooting that left at least 12 people wounded Saturday night.

item.title: At least 12 shot near Ohio festival, gunmen still at large

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: A judge has dropped the murder charge against Arkansas sheriff candidate Aaron Spencer, who killed a man accused of sexually abusing his daughter.

media:content.media:text: Aaron Spencer stands outside during sunset
media:content.media:description: Arkansas county sheriff candidate Aaron Spencer. The murder charge against him for killing a 67-year-old was dismissed.

item.title: Judge drops murder charge against Arkansas sheriff candidate who killed a man

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Terry Bollea — known professionally as Hulk Hogan — died of natural causes, according to a report released Friday by the Clearwater Police Department, concluding its investigation into the July 2025 death of the legendary pro wrestler.

media:content.media:text: Hulk Hogan speaks on stage while pointing
media:content.media:description: Hulk Hogan during the Republican National Convention in Milwaukee in 2024.

item.title: Florida police share final report on Hulk Hogan’s cause of death

item.dc:creator: Claudio Lavanga
Mirna Alsharif
item.description: The United States has the papal blessing for the World Cup.

media:content.media:text: Pope Leo XIV.
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican on June 3, 2026.

item.title: Pope Leo says he ‘certainly’ will support the U.S. in the World Cup

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: We’re tracking the best early Amazon Prime Day 2026 deals, including discounts on tech, home, beauty, kitchen products and more before the four-day sale begins.

media:content.media:text: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.
media:content.media:description: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.

media:content.media:text: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.
media:content.media:description: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.

item.title: The 51+ best early Amazon Prime Day 2026 deals you can shop now: Bissell, Le Creuset, Levi’s, and more

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: Target just announced its dates for Target Circle Week also called Target Circle Deal Days. The sale event will take place fromTuesday, June 23 to Friday, June 26.

media:content.media:text: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.
media:content.media:description: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.

media:content.media:text: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.
media:content.media:description: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.

item.title: Target just announced dates for Target Circle Week: Here’s everything you should know
